tweets.text:
The Morning Minute (7.13)

Powered by @yeet 

⏰Top News:
-Crypto majors fall 1-3% after overnight selloff; BTC at $63k
-BTC ETFs flip to net inflows after brutal 2-month stretch
-NEAR activated its v2.13.0 mainnet upgrade
-Zcash confirmed July 28 as the date for its Ironwood upgrade
-Robinhood Chain sees $2B+ in DEX volume over weekend with over 800k active addresses

🌎 Macro Crypto and Markets
-Crypto majors are down 1-3% after an overnight selloff; BTC -1% at $63k; ETH -1% at $1,780; SOL -1% at $76; HYPE -3% at $65
-JTO (+13%), PUMP (+8%) and PYTH (+6%) led top movers; ARB (+18%) and ZEC (+16%) were biggest winners of the week
-Oil +3.5% at $74; Gold -1% at $4,070
-Stock futures are red as the Iran War escalates again; DOW flat, Nasdaq -0.9%

-Bitcoin’s BIP-110 soft fork faces an early-August deadline with miner support at zero, the proposal to cap non-financial data on the chain never having drawn above 1%

-Meta’s chief data officer called agentic commerce the potential “next tier of business” for the company, saying stablecoins are central to a future without physical wallets, with over a million weekly active businesses already using Meta’s AI agents

Corporate Treasuries & ETFs
-The Bitcoin ETFs saw $197M in net inflows last week including $90M on Friday; the ETH ETFs saw $84M in inflows, including $18M on Friday

Meme Coin Tracker
-Meme leaders were mostly red on the week down 5-10%; DOGE -6%, SHIB -4%, PEPE +2%, PENGU -10%, TRUMP -7%, BONK -16%
-Robinhood chain memes rebounded after a weekend selloff; Cashcat -6% to $160M, Juggernaut +50% to $15M, Hoodrat +45% to $12M and RMI +220x to $7M
-ANSEM rebounded 25% to $250M leading Solana movers; TripleT +20% and febu +60% were other notable Solana movers

💰 Token, Airdrop & Protocol Tracker
-Robinhood Chain sees over $2B in DEX volume over the weekend with over 800k active addresses
-NEAR activated its v2.13.0 mainnet upgrade, adding post-quantum signature support, automatic shard scaling, and a redesigned gas payment system
-Zcash confirmed July 28 as the date for its Ironwood upgrade
-Backpack launched 24/7 trading of tokenized US stocks

🚚 What is happening in NFTs?
-NFT leaders were mixed; Punks even at 32.4 ETH, BAYC -4% at 8.9 ETH, Pudgy -3% at 4.4 ETH; Hypurr’s -6% at 287 HYPE
-Murakami Flowers (+23%) and Squiggles (+13%) led top movers
-New Robinhood NFT sets jumped including Robbin Hood Babies (+160%) and Onchain Hoodies (+27%)

tweets.text:
BREAKING: Total US federal debt is now up to a record $39.4 trillion, rising +$3.2 trillion over the last 12 months.

Since 2020, US federal debt is now up a massive +$16.3 trillion.

This marks a +$2.5 trillion average annual increase, or +$209 billion per month.

At this pace, total US debt will surge to $50.0 trillion before 2030.

The US debt crisis has no end in sight.

tweets.text:
Hedge funds are piling into US semiconductor stocks:

Last week, hedge funds purchased the most US semiconductor stocks in at least 3.5 years.

This follows the 2 largest consecutive weekly sales since June 2024.

As a result, semiconductor stocks now account for 10% of total hedge fund exposure.

This percentage is twice as high as during the same period last year.

However, this remains below the peak of 14% recorded in May.

Hedge funds are betting the semiconductor selloff is already over.

tweets.text:
BREAKING: President Trump says the US is reinstating its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz for Iranian ships and customers.

Trump says the US will now be known as "The Guardian of the Strait of Hormuz" and will be "reimbursed" at a rate of 20% on all cargo shipped.

It appears the US is now imposing a blockade and fees to transit the Strait of Hormuz.
tweets.full_text:

tweets.text:
BREAKING: The UAE is planning to build a new port and container terminal on the country's east cost to bypass the Strait of Hormuz, per FT.

Details include:

1. Dubai's DP World is in talks to develop a new multipurpose port in the coastal area of Fujairah

2. The new project would allow containers to enter and leave the country without having to pass through the Strait of Hormuz

3. Shipments would then be moved on trucks overland to Dubai, Abu Dhabi, and neighboring Gulf countries

The Iran War continues to reshape global trade.

tweets.text:
🇨🇳 China is beginning to replicate in wind power what it has already achieved in solar panels, batteries and electric vehicles: turning domestic industrial dominance into a global offensive.

📈 International orders secured by Chinese turbine manufacturers increased from 1.3 GW in 2021 to 14.3 GW in 2025, an elevenfold rise in just four years. Obviously, China's main advantage remains price. Thanks to highly integrated supply chains, enormous domestic volumes and intense competition within China, they can offer cheaper turbines than their Western rivals. For developers, particularly in emerging markets, this reduces construction costs, improves project economics and can make viable projects that might otherwise never have been built.

➡️ The most important signal is their growing credibility. A wind turbine must operate for twenty or thirty years with reliable warranties, maintenance, spare parts and after-sales support. By winning major contracts, Chinese manufacturers are gradually becoming more bankable in the eyes of developers, banks and insurers.

⚠️ Some caution is still necessary though. Goldwind and Envision accounted for around 80% of new international orders won by Chinese manufacturers in 2025 while the Saudi contract weighs heavily in the overall figures, so growth remains highly concentrated. In addition, Western manufacturers still retain major advantages in maintenance, reputation and local presence. In Europe and the United States, concerns around cybersecurity, strategic autonomy and local-content requirements are also likely to slow Chinese expansion.

A new price war is taking shape and for Europe the dilemma will be the same as in solar. Accelerate the transition with cheaper Chinese equipment, or protect a strategic domestic industry.

https://t.co/MPickDU0EZ
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Great new report from @BloombergNEF 

Chinese wind turbine exports are surging all around the world. And increasingly these companies are expanding their manufacturing footprint abroad. https://t.co/FSB9thuWCk

tweets.text: On this day in 2012, JPMorgan Chase reported second-quarter earnings and revealed that the "London Whale" trading losses had ballooned to $5.8 billion, nearly triple the $2 billion that CEO Jamie Dimon had acknowledged two months earlier. The trades were placed by Bruno Iksil, a derivatives trader in JPMorgan's Chief Investment Office in London who had built such enormous positions in credit default swaps that hedge funds across the market could see him moving prices. Dimon had dismissed the initial reports as a "tempest in a teapot" on an April earnings call, a quote that became one of the most mocked lines in modern Wall Street history. By year-end, total losses hit $6.2 billion, and JPMorgan paid $920 million in fines to U.S. and U.K. regulators. It was the largest trading loss at a single U.S. bank since the 2008 financial crisis.
tweets.full_text: On this day in 2012, JPMorgan Chase reported second-quarter earnings and revealed that the "London Whale" trading losses had ballooned to $5.8 billion, nearly triple the $2 billion that CEO Jamie Dimon had acknowledged two months earlier. The trades were placed by Bruno Iksil, a derivatives trader in JPMorgan's Chief Investment Office in London who had built such enormous positions in credit default swaps that hedge funds across the market could see him moving prices. Dimon had dismissed the initial reports as a "tempest in a teapot" on an April earnings call, a quote that became one of the most mocked lines in modern Wall Street history. By year-end, total losses hit $6.2 billion, and JPMorgan paid $920 million in fines to U.S. and U.K. regulators. It was the largest trading loss at a single U.S. bank since the 2008 financial crisis.

tweets.text:
📈 The bond market is telling us that financing the AI race is becoming significantly more expensive, even for the most powerful companies in the world.

📊 The G-spread on a basket of bonds issued by hyperscalers has climbed to around 148 basis points. Investors are now demanding nearly 1.5 percentage points of additional yield over comparable government bonds to lend to the technology giants. This means that their debt is no longer being absorbed as easily as before.

➡️ The bond market is beginning to struggle with the sheer volume of issuance required to finance AI. However, the move also reflects a more fundamental concern. Hyperscalers are gradually becoming infrastructure builders with capital requirements increasingly similar to those of industrial or energy companies. The market is not necessarily questioning the usefulness of AI, but is asking how long it will take to monetise these investments and whether every additional dollar spent will generate as much profit as the previous one.

⚠️ Equity investors focus on future growth and technological potential while credit investors focus on debt, free cash flow and financial discipline. The credit market can therefore begin to deteriorate even while technology stocks continue to rise.

Part of the widening is probably due to technical market saturation but if spreads continue to widen while equity markets remain strong, it would suggest that investors are beginning to genuinely question the financial returns of this infrastructure race.

https://t.co/WEgosDm2NS

tweets.text:
🇰🇷 S. Korea Govt Plans Record 2027 Spending, Future Response Fund - Bloomberg
- South Korea plans a record spending of more than 800t won in 2027 budget while the nation expects a record national tax revenue of more than 500t won, Budget Minister Park Hong-geun says at a government meeting.
- 2027 national tax revenue of more than 500t won exceeds previous government estimate of 412t won
- Excess tax revenue to be allocated to the Future Response Fund; fund to invest in youth, future growth engines, regional development and talent
- President Lee Jae Myung says earlier excess tax revenue will come from boom in chip industry

Robinhood Chain produces a block every 100 ms.

We measure transaction-submission latency from probes around the world.

 Ohio: 3 ms
 Virginia: 18 ms
 Chicago: 27 ms
 London: 91 ms
 Tokyo: 140 ms
 Sydney: 200 ms

Proximity provides up to 2 blocks worth of edge. Traders in the US could be frontrunning those across the world with ease.

 Live map for all chains → https://t.co/g6s07ftrq8

thread_tweets.text:
Rent control is great politics and terrible economics.

It rewards existing tenants while making housing scarcer and more expensive for the next generation.

You can cap rents, but you can’t repeal supply and demand.

tweets.text: Hello from Germany, where the Rhine’s Kaub chokepoint is at its lowest water level for this time of year in decades. Barges can carry less than 20% of normal loads, diesel freight rates from Rotterdam to Karlsruhe jumped 56% – and another heat wave is turning drought into a supply-chain shock. https://t.co/WjiqQQna68

tweets.text:
Brazilian stocks finally brought friends.

For 52 sessions, fewer than half of Ibovespa stocks traded above their 50-day averages.
After similar breaks, local currency returns were positive every time over the next 1 to 5 months, with no drawdown worse than 5%.

For $EWZ, currency swings have meant double-digit drawdowns.

Read full analysis: https://t.co/wX5yLsoGWV
